error.signature: ClusterBlockException: blocked by: [FORBIDDEN/9/index read-only / allow delete (api)]; [FORBIDDEN/13/cluster read-only / allow delete (api)]

verdict.summary: Cluster-level write block active. Triggered by disk watermarks, manual settings, or metadata write block during upgrades.

dead_ends.action: Force delete indices to free space while blocks are active
dead_ends.why_fails: Read-only-allow-delete blocks permit deletion, but cluster-level blocks may prevent even that. Clear the block first.

dead_ends.action: Restart nodes to clear the block
dead_ends.why_fails: Blocks set via persistent settings survive restarts. The block must be cleared via the cluster settings API.

workarounds.action: Clear the cluster read-only block via settings API
Free disk space to clear auto-applied flood watermark blocks
workarounds.how: Check: GET _cat/allocation?v  # then delete old indices or add disk

workarounds.action: Check if upgrade or snapshot operation set a temporary metadata block
